pattern: Simulate the distribution rule: start arr1 with nums[0] and arr2 with nums[1], then for each subsequent element compare the last elements of the two arrays to decide which gets the next value.
hints: Initialize arr1 = [nums[0]] and arr2 = [nums[1]], then process nums[2..n-1] one at a time.
If the last element of arr1 is greater than the last element of arr2, append the current element to arr1; otherwise append to arr2.
The result is arr1 concatenated with arr2.

edge_inputs.description: minimum length 3, first > second so third goes to arr1
first < second so everything beyond goes to arr2
all elements strictly decreasing after first two
all distinct values — check each routing step carefully
elements are distinct as required, but routing ping-pongs

stress.description: random arrays of distinct values length 3–50
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = size
    vals = rng.sample(range(1, 101), min(n, 100))
    return [vals]
